3.name: Lesson 3: Present Simple & General Actions (一般動詞・時間)

questions.question: Do you live in Tokyo? Do you live in Tokyo?
questions.expectedAnswer: No, I don't live in Tokyo, but I live in Osaka.

questions.question: Do you speak English? Do you speak English?
questions.expectedAnswer: Yes, I speak English.

questions.question: Do you like coffee? Do you like coffee?
questions.expectedAnswer: No, I don't like coffee, but I like tea.

questions.question: Do you come to school by car? Do you come to school by car?
questions.expectedAnswer: No, I don't come to school by car, but I come by train.

questions.question: Is it ten o'clock now? Is it ten o'clock now?
questions.expectedAnswer: Yes, it's ten o'clock now.

questions.question: Do you wear a clock on your wrist? Do you wear a clock on your wrist?
questions.expectedAnswer: No, I don't wear a clock on my wrist, but I wear a watch.

questions.question: Does a day have twenty hours? Does a day have twenty hours?
questions.expectedAnswer: No, a day doesn't have twenty hours, but it has twenty-four hours.

questions.question: Does an hour have sixty minutes? Does an hour have sixty minutes?
questions.expectedAnswer: Yes, an hour has sixty minutes.

questions.question: Does a minute have thirty seconds? Does a minute have thirty seconds?
questions.expectedAnswer: No, a minute doesn't have thirty seconds, but it has sixty seconds.

questions.question: Do you go to bed early every day? Do you go to bed early every day?
questions.expectedAnswer: Yes, I go to bed early every day.

questions.question: Do you wear a watch? Do you wear a watch?
questions.expectedAnswer: Yes, I wear a watch.

questions.question: Do you live in a big city? Do you live in a big city?
questions.expectedAnswer: No, I don't live in a big city, but I live in a small town.

questions.question: Does a dog speak English? Does a dog speak English?
questions.expectedAnswer: No, a dog doesn't speak English, but it barks.

questions.question: Do you like to eat pizza? Do you like to eat pizza?
questions.expectedAnswer: Yes, I like to eat pizza.

questions.question: Do you come to school by bus? Do you come to school by bus?
questions.expectedAnswer: No, I don't come to school by bus, but I come by train.

questions.question: Does a day have fifty hours? Does a day have fifty hours?
questions.expectedAnswer: No, a day doesn't have fifty hours, but it has twenty-four hours.

questions.question: Do we go to sleep in the morning? Do we go to sleep in the morning?
questions.expectedAnswer: No, we don't go to sleep in the morning, but we go to sleep at night.

questions.question: Does a minute have sixty seconds? Does a minute have sixty seconds?
questions.expectedAnswer: Yes, a minute has sixty seconds.

questions.question: Does the sun come up in the morning? Does the sun come up in the morning?
questions.expectedAnswer: Yes, the sun comes up in the morning.

3.name: Stage 3: Pre-Intermediate

2.name: Lesson 2: Superlatives & Frequency Adverbs (最上級・頻度)

questions.question: Is London the biggest city in Europe? Is London the biggest city in Europe?
questions.expectedAnswer: No, London isn't the biggest city in Europe, but Moscow's the biggest city in Europe.

questions.question: What's the most expensive item in your bag? What's the most expensive item in your bag?
questions.expectedAnswer: My phone's the most expensive item in my bag.

questions.question: Do you always eat sugar with your coffee? Do you always eat sugar with your coffee?
questions.expectedAnswer: No, I don't always eat sugar with my coffee, but I never eat sugar with my coffee.

questions.question: Do you sometimes eat out at a restaurant? Do you sometimes eat out at a restaurant?
questions.expectedAnswer: Yes, I sometimes eat out at a restaurant.

questions.question: Is your shirt the most expensive item of clothing you're wearing? Is your shirt the most expensive item of clothing you're wearing?
questions.expectedAnswer: No, my shirt isn't the most expensive item of clothing I'm wearing, but my shoes are.

questions.question: Do you rarely watch TV? Do you rarely watch TV?
questions.expectedAnswer: Yes, I rarely watch TV.

questions.question: Is this chair the biggest object in this room? Is this chair the biggest object in this room?
questions.expectedAnswer: No, this chair isn't the biggest object in this room, but the table's.

questions.question: Do you always drink water with your meals? Do you always drink water with your meals?
questions.expectedAnswer: No, I don't always drink water with my meals, but I sometimes drink water with my meals.

questions.expectedAnswer: Practice's the most important thing for learning English.

questions.expectedAnswer: No, I don't always go to bed before midnight, but I sometimes go to bed before midnight.

questions.question: Is London the biggest city in the world? Is London the biggest city in the world?
questions.expectedAnswer: No, London isn't the biggest city in the world, but Tokyo is.

questions.question: Is a small car the most expensive type of car? Is a small car the most expensive type of car?

questions.question: Do you always come to school by car? Do you always come to school by car?

questions.question: Do you sometimes eat meat for breakfast? Do you sometimes eat meat for breakfast?

questions.question: Do you always eat sugar with your tea? Do you always eat sugar with your tea?
questions.expectedAnswer: No, I don't always eat sugar with my tea, but I rarely eat it.

questions.question: Is a cat the biggest animal in the world? Is a cat the biggest animal in the world?

questions.question: Do you rarely go to the cinema? Do you rarely go to the cinema?
questions.expectedAnswer: Yes, I rarely go to the cinema.

questions.question: Is water the most expensive drink? Is water the most expensive drink?

questions.question: Do you always wear a coat in summer? Do you always wear a coat in summer?
questions.expectedAnswer: No, I don't always wear a coat in summer, but I wear one in winter.

questions.question: Do you sometimes speak English at home? Do you sometimes speak English at home?
questions.expectedAnswer: Yes, I sometimes speak English at home.

3.name: Lesson 3: Imperatives & Quantity (命令形・数量詞)

questions.question: Am I telling you to stand up? Am I telling you to stand up?
questions.expectedAnswer: No, you aren't telling me to stand up, but you're telling me to sit down.

questions.question: Can we count the money? Can we count the money?
questions.expectedAnswer: No, we can't count the money, but we can count the coins.

questions.question: Do you have many pens? Do you have many pens?
questions.expectedAnswer: No, I don't have many pens, but I've got a few.

questions.question: Is there much water in the sea? Is there much water in the sea?

questions.expectedAnswer: No, she doesn't tell him to open the door, but she tells him to close the door.

questions.expectedAnswer: No, there aren't few people in this city, but there are many.

4.name: Stage 4: Intermediate-Intro
4.description: 過去形・不規則動詞、現在完了形、条件文（if）
